---
title: "figure4_heatmap"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "11/12/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)
library(Hmisc)
library(writexl)

library(tidygraph)
library(ppcor)

library(reshape2)
library(ComplexHeatmap)

```


#demographic
```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
species = read_xlsx("data/species_866.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")

food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(across(c("Mushrooms", "Papaya", "Orange", "Chapati"), as.numeric))
food_data
nutrition_data
species
metadata
```

```{r}
colnames(species)[1] = "Participant_ID"
colnames(metadata)[1] = "Participant_ID"
threshold <- nrow(species) * 0.5

species_filtered1 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > nrow(species) * 0.2)
species_filtered1
species_filtered2 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)

#abundance_threshold <- 0.0001
#species_filtered2 <- species_filtered %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x) > (abundance_threshold * sum(species)))
#species_filtered2
species_filtered2 <- species_filtered2[, !names(species_filtered2) %in% c("unclassified", "others", "Homo sapiens")]
#species_filtered2$`Homo sapiens`
#species_filtered2
food_data[is.na(food_data)] <- 0
#food_data <- data.frame(lapply(food_data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
nutrition_data[is.na(nutrition_data)] <- 0
```


```{r}
unique_species <- read.csv("figure/unique_significant_species.csv")
unique_species
sig_species <- species %>%
  select("Participant_ID", all_of(unique_species$species))
```


```{r}
food_data
nutrition_data
sig_species
metadata
```
```{r}
merged_data <- food_data %>%
  inner_join(sig_species, by = "Participant_ID") %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

# 2. Calculate partial correlations adjusting for confounders
# Initialize matrices to store correlations and p-values
species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
food_cols <- colnames(food_data)[colnames(food_data) != "Participant_ID"]
cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(food_cols))
p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(food_cols))

for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
  for(j in 1:length(food_cols)) {
    # Create dummy variables for ethnicity using the correct column name
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      species = rank(merged_data[[species_cols[i]]]),  
      food = rank(merged_data[[food_cols[j]]]),        
      gender = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
      age = rank(merged_data$FREG8_Age),              
      bmi = rank(merged_data$BMI),                    
      ethnic_C = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C"),  # Updated column name
      ethnic_I = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I"),
      ethnic_M = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M")
    )
    
    pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$food, 
                            temp_data[,c("gender", "age", "bmi", 
                                       "ethnic_C", "ethnic_I", "ethnic_M")])
    
    cor_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$estimate
    p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
  }
}

# Convert matrices to data frames
rownames(cor_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(cor_matrix) <- food_cols
rownames(p_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(p_matrix) <- food_cols

# Create annotation for significant correlations
sig_matrix <- p_matrix < 0.05
stars <- ifelse(sig_matrix, "*", "")
```

```{r}
species_dist <- dist(cor_matrix)
species_clust <- hclust(species_dist)
species_order <- species_clust$labels[species_clust$order]

# For food groups (columns)
food_dist <- dist(t(cor_matrix))
food_clust <- hclust(food_dist)
food_order <- food_clust$labels[food_clust$order]

# Convert to long format with ordered factors
melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
colnames(melted_cor) <- c("Species", "Food", "Correlation")
melted_cor$Significant <- melted_p$value < 0.05

# Set the order based on clustering
melted_cor$Species <- factor(melted_cor$Species, levels = species_order)
melted_cor$Food <- factor(melted_cor$Food, levels = food_order)

# Create custom color palette
extended_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)

# Create the plot with italicized species names
ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Food, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(extended_palette), 
                      limits = c(-0.2, 0.2),
                      oob = scales::squish) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),  # Italicize species names
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Spearman Correlations between Species and Food Groups",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and BMI\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Food Groups",
    y = "Species"
  )
ggsave("figure/figure4_correlation_heatmap.png", width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```
```{r}
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(grid)
library(circlize)
library(RColorBrewer)
# Clip the correlation values to [-0.2, 0.2]
clipped_cor_matrix <- cor_matrix
clipped_cor_matrix[clipped_cor_matrix > 0.2] <- 0.2
clipped_cor_matrix[clipped_cor_matrix < -0.2] <- -0.2

#display.brewer.pal(11, "RdBu")  # This will show us the actual colors

rd_bu_colors <- rev(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))

# Modify color function to use full RdBu palette evenly over the actual data range
col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-0.2, 0, 0.2), rd_bu_colors[c(1, 6, 11)])

# Create the matrix of significance stars
star_matrix <- matrix("", nrow = nrow(cor_matrix), ncol = ncol(cor_matrix))
star_matrix[p_matrix < 0.05] <- "*"

# Create the heatmap using the clipped matrix
ht <- Heatmap(clipped_cor_matrix,
        name = "Correlation",
        col = col_fun,
        cluster_rows = TRUE,
        cluster_columns = TRUE,
        row_names_gp = gpar(fontface = "italic"),  # Italic species names
        column_names_rot = 45,  # Rotate column names
        cell_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
          if(star_matrix[i, j] == "*")
            grid.text("*", x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 14))
        },
        heatmap_legend_param = list(
          title = "Spearman\nCorrelation",
          at = seq(-0.2, 0.2, by = 0.1)
        ),
        column_title = "Food Groups",
        row_title = "Species",
        column_title_side = "bottom",
        show_row_dend = TRUE,  # Show row dendrogram
        show_column_dend = TRUE  # Show column dendrogram
)
ht
# Save the plot
png("figure/figure2_correlation_heatmap_with_dendrograms.png",
    width = 12, height = 10, units = "in", res = 300)
draw(ht, padding = unit(c(2, 2, 2, 2), "cm"))
dev.off()
```



```{r}
ggsave("figure/figure4_correlation_heatmap.png", width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# Print the colors we're using
extended_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)
print("Colors at positions 1, 50, 100:")
print(extended_palette[c(1, 50, 100)])
print("Colors after reversing:")
print(rev(extended_palette[c(1, 50, 100)]))

# Try different ways of creating the color mapping
# Original way
col_fun1 <- colorRamp2(c(-0.2, 0, 0.2), rev(extended_palette[c(1, 50, 100)]))

# Alternative way - using the palette directly
col_fun2 <- colorRamp2(c(-0.2, 0, 0.2), c("#67001F", "#F7F7F7", "#053061"))

# Print a few sample transformations
test_values <- c(-0.2, -0.1, 0, 0.1, 0.2)
print("Sample colors for test values:")
print(col_fun1(test_values))
display.brewer.pal(11, "RdBu")
```
